title: Qripto & KNYT Persona iQube Schemas + LinkedIn / MetaMask Integration Spec
description: Complete schema and integration specification for the dual-persona iQube system in Aigent Nakamoto. Each persona is a tri-partite iQube composed of (1) metaQube — public provenance/risk metadata, (2) blakQube — encrypted private user data, and (3) tokenQube — access control / ownership / on-chain settlement. This document also specifies how the LinkedIn OAuth integration and the MetaMask Web3 wallet integration populate the blakQube fields for each persona type, including the KNYT $COYN ERC-20 balance ingestion pipeline.

iQubeOntology.principle: Every persona iQube is cryptographically composed of three entangled subdivisions. Public metadata lives in the metaQube, encrypted PII lives in the blakQube, and access keys + on-chain settlement live in the tokenQube.

metaQube.purpose: Public provenance, risk scoring, usage rights, and routing metadata. Always readable; never contains PII.
metaQube.storage: Static client-side definition (src/components/settings/QubeData.tsx) — surfaced in the UI's MetaQubeHeader.
metaQube.scoringScale: 0–10 integer or one decimal. Risk-Score is computed/curated as a function of Sensitivity vs Verifiability+Accuracy.

blakQube.purpose: Encrypted private payload — the actual user PII, wallet addresses, social handles, and KNYT asset holdings.
blakQube.storage: Supabase Postgres tables `knyt_personas` and `qripto_personas`, gated by per-user RLS (auth.uid() = user_id).
blakQube.encryptionLevel: AES-256 at rest; TLS in transit; row-level security enforced by Postgres.

tokenQube.purpose: Ownership, access grants, network settings, and on-chain provenance for the iQube.
tokenQube.storage: On-chain (Ethereum mainnet for KNYT $COYN ERC-20 settlement) + `user_connections` table for wallet binding + UI in src/components/settings/tokenqube/

qriptoPersona.intendedUser: Web3 / crypto-native user; lighter PII footprint, focused on wallet identity and social/dev presence.

metaQube.iQube-Use: For personalized cryptocurrency recommendations and portfolio analysis

identity.Email: text — populated from LinkedIn email or auth.email

crypto.Chain-IDs: text[] — chains the user holds assets on
crypto.Wallets-of-Interest: text[] — auto-appends 'MetaMask' on wallet connect, 'ThirdWeb' on ThirdWeb connect
crypto.Web3-Interests: text[] — derived from LinkedIn industry + skills keyword filter

tokenQube.accessControl: Self-sovereign — only the auth.uid() owner can decrypt blakQube fields
tokenQube.walletBinding: user_connections row with service='wallet', signed message verifies ownership

qriptoPersona.personaDetectionHeuristic: A persona is detected as Qripto when KNYT-specific fields (KNYT-COYN-Owned, KNYT-ID, OM-Member-Since, Total-Invested, Metaiye-Shares-Owned) are absent.

knytPersona.intendedUser: metaKnyts ecosystem member; richer PII footprint, includes investment history, OM tier status, and on-chain $KNYT COYN holdings.

metaQube.iQube-Use: For KNYT ecosystem profile management and reward tracking

investmentAndMembership.Total-Invested: text — numeric (USD), formatted with $ in UI

knytAssetHoldings.KNYT-COYN-Owned: text — populated from on-chain ERC-20 balanceOf via MetaMask provider (KNYT_TOKEN_CONFIG.address = 0xe53dad36cd0A8EdC656448CE7912bba72beBECb4)

fields.linkedin_connected: boolean — set true when LinkedIn OAuth completes
fields.metamask_connected: boolean — set true when wallet signature verified
fields.data_completed: boolean — set true when all required blakQube fields filled

rewardSystem.rule: User earns 2,800 Satoshi reward for completing LinkedIn + MetaMask + full data.

knytPersona.personaDetectionHeuristic: A persona is detected as KNYT when ANY of these fields exist on the persona object: 'KNYT-COYN-Owned', 'KNYT-ID', 'OM-Member-Since', 'Total-Invested', 'Metaiye-Shares-Owned'. Used by src/services/blakqube/connection-processors.ts to gate KNYT-COYN-Owned writes.

personaActivation.mechanism: Client-side localStorage flags + window CustomEvent dispatch. Mutually exclusive between KNYT and Qripto.
events: iqubeToggle — { iqubeId: 'KNYT Persona' | 'Qripto Persona', active: boolean }
personaActivation.mutualExclusion: src/pages/Settings.tsx#toggleQubeActive — activating one persona deactivates the other.

linkedInIntegration.purpose: Populate identity (First-Name, Last-Name, Profession, Local-City), social (LinkedIn-ID, LinkedIn-Profile-URL, Email), and inferred Web3-Interests fields in the blakQube of whichever persona is active.

step1_initiate.behavior: Generates state UUID, builds LinkedIn authorization URL, returns { authUrl, state, redirectUri } to client.

step2_userConsent.behavior: Browser navigates to authUrl; user authorizes; LinkedIn redirects to redirectUri with ?code=...&state=...

actions: Exchange authorization code for access_token at https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken (15s timeout)
Fetch basic profile from https://api.linkedin.com/v2/userinfo → { sub, given_name, family_name, email, picture }
Smart client-origin detection from Referer / Origin headers (fallback: https://nakamoto.aigentz.me)

fieldMapping.Web3-Interests: Append profile.industryName if it matches keywords [blockchain, crypto, cryptocurrency, web3, defi, nft, bitcoin, ethereum, fintech, digital assets, smart contracts, dapp, decentralized, tokenization]; also append profile.skills filtered for [blockchain, crypto, web3, smart contract, solidity, defi, nft, ethereum, bitcoin]

step5_blakQubePopulation.writeStrategy: Non-destructive — only fills empty fields; LinkedIn-ID and LinkedIn-Profile-URL always overwrite.

errorHandling.timeouts: 15s for token exchange, 10s for profile fetches
errorHandling.fallbackEndpoint: If /v2/me projection fails, falls back to /v2/people/(id:~)?projection=(...)
errorHandling.userFacingErrors: HTML error page with auto-redirect to /oauth-callback?service=linkedin&error=<reason> after 3s

metaMaskIntegration.purpose: (1) Bind an EVM wallet to the user. (2) Populate EVM-Public-Key in blakQube. (3) For KNYT personas only — fetch on-chain $KNYT COYN ERC-20 balance and write it to KNYT-COYN-Owned.

flow.step1_providerCheck: Verify window.ethereum exists. Reject with toast if MetaMask not installed.

step2_networkValidation.rule: Must be on Ethereum Mainnet (chainId 0x1). If not, calls switchToMainnet() → wallet_switchEthereumChain.
step2_networkValidation.rationale: KNYT $COYN contract lives on mainnet; off-chain reads would return zero or revert.

step4_signatureVerification.messageTemplate:
Please sign this message to verify your wallet ownership.

Wallet: <address>
Timestamp: <ISO>
step4_signatureVerification.purpose: Proves the user controls the private key — anti-spoofing for the wallet binding.

step5_knytBalanceFetch.rpc: window.ethereum.request({ method: 'eth_call', params: [{ to: contractAddress, data: callData }, 'latest'] })
step5_knytBalanceFetch.parsing: BigInt(0x + hex result) / 10^18 → human-readable KNYT amount

caching.deduplication: pendingBalanceFetches map prevents concurrent duplicate RPC calls per address
caching.fallback: If a fresh fetch fails, return cached value if within 90s (3× cache duration)

step5_knytBalanceFetch.monitoring: knytTokenService.monitorTransactions() listens for accountsChanged + chainChanged and re-fetches balance on changes.

writes.Tokens-of-Interest: Append any of [ETH, BTC, USDC, USDT, KNYT] not yet present
writes.KNYT-COYN-Owned: ONLY written if persona is detected as KNYT (see personaDetectionHeuristic). Value = connection_data.knytTokenBalance.formatted (e.g. '1,250 KNYT').

step7_blakQubeSync.auditMetadata: Logged (not persisted): { lastBalanceUpdate, balanceSource: 'wallet_connection', transactionHash, rawBalance }

flow.step8_eventDispatch: Dispatches CustomEvents: privateDataUpdated, personaDataUpdated, balanceUpdated, walletDataRefreshed — UI components subscribe to refresh.

addTokenToWallet.rpc: wallet_watchAsset with { type: 'ERC20', options: { address, symbol: 'KNYT', decimals: 18 } }
addTokenToWallet.purpose: One-click 'Add KNYT to MetaMask' UX so users see the token in their wallet.

refreshAndUpdateMethods: walletConnectionService.updateWalletWithKnytBalance() — called on Settings page mount; cached read.
walletConnectionService.refreshKnytBalance() — clears cache and forces fresh on-chain read.

scenario_linkedIn: 1. User clicks 'Connect LinkedIn' in Settings → ConnectionsTab.
3. Client invokes connect-linkedin edge function → receives authUrl, state.
4. Browser redirects to LinkedIn → user consents → LinkedIn redirects to oauth-callback-linkedin edge function.
5. Edge function exchanges code, fetches /v2/userinfo + /v2/me, builds connection_data, 302 redirects to ${clientOrigin}/oauth-callback?service=linkedin&success=true&connection_data=<urlencoded>.
6. OAuthCallback.tsx parses connection_data, upserts into user_connections (service='linkedin').
7. blakQubeService.updatePersonaFromConnections('knyt'|'qripto') runs processLinkedInConnection() against the active persona's row in knyt_personas / qripto_personas.
8. Persona row updated → personaDataSync notifies subscribers → UserPersonaDisplay refreshes.
scenario_metaMask: 2. Network validated (Ethereum Mainnet) → switch if needed.
4. knytTokenService.getTokenBalance(address) → eth_call balanceOf on KNYT contract → parse BigInt → cache.
5. Upsert user_connections row with { address, signature, knytTokenBalance }.
7. processWalletConnection sets EVM-Public-Key on both; sets KNYT-COYN-Owned ONLY on KNYT persona (heuristic).
8. CustomEvents dispatched → KnytBalanceDisplay, BlakQubeSection, MetaQubeHeader re-render.

supabaseSchemaDDL.qripto_personas_excerpt: CREATE TABLE qripto_personas ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid NOT NULL, "First-Name" text DEFAULT '', "Last-Name" text DEFAULT '', "Qripto-ID" text DEFAULT '', "Profession" text DEFAULT '', "Local-City" text DEFAULT '', "Email" text DEFAULT '', "EVM-Public-Key" text DEFAULT '', "BTC-Public-Key" text DEFAULT '', "Chain-IDs" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "Wallets-of-Interest" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "Web3-Interests" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "Tokens-of-Interest" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "LinkedIn-ID" text DEFAULT '', "LinkedIn-Profile-URL" text DEFAULT '', "Twitter-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "Telegram-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "Discord-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "Instagram-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "GitHub-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "YouTube-ID" text DEFAULT '', "Facebook-ID" text DEFAULT '', "TikTok-Handle" text DEFAULT '', profile_image_url text DEFAULT '', created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now() );
supabaseSchemaDDL.knyt_personas_excerpt: CREATE TABLE knyt_personas ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid NOT NULL, "First-Name" text DEFAULT '', "Last-Name" text DEFAULT '', "KNYT-ID" text DEFAULT '', "Profession" text DEFAULT '', "Local-City" text DEFAULT '', "Email" text DEFAULT '', "Phone-Number" text DEFAULT '', "Age" text DEFAULT '', "Address" text DEFAULT '', "EVM-Public-Key" text DEFAULT '', "BTC-Public-Key" text DEFAULT '', "ThirdWeb-Public-Key" text DEFAULT '', "MetaKeep-Public-Key" text DEFAULT '', "Chain-IDs" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "Wallets-of-Interest" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "Web3-Interests" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "Tokens-of-Interest" text[] DEFAULT '{}', "LinkedIn-ID" text DEFAULT '', "LinkedIn-Profile-URL" text DEFAULT '', "Twitter-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "Telegram-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "Discord-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "Instagram-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "YouTube-ID" text DEFAULT '', "Facebook-ID" text DEFAULT '', "TikTok-Handle" text DEFAULT '', "OM-Member-Since" text DEFAULT '', "OM-Tier-Status" text DEFAULT '', "Metaiye-Shares-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "Total-Invested" text DEFAULT '', "KNYT-COYN-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "Motion-Comics-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "Paper-Comics-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "Digital-Comics-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "KNYT-Posters-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "KNYT-Cards-Owned" text DEFAULT '', "Characters-Owned" text DEFAULT '', profile_image_url text DEFAULT '', created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now() );
supabaseSchemaDDL.user_connections: CREATE TABLE user_connections ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid NOT NULL, service text NOT NULL, connection_data jsonb, connected_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now() ); -- service ∈ { 'linkedin','wallet','thirdweb','twitter','telegram','discord','instagram','facebook','youtube','tiktok','luma' }
supabaseSchemaDDL.knyt_persona_rewards: CREATE TABLE knyt_persona_rewards ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid NOT NULL, linkedin_connected boolean DEFAULT false, metamask_connected boolean DEFAULT false, data_completed boolean DEFAULT false, reward_amount integer DEFAULT 2800, reward_claimed boolean DEFAULT false, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now() );

minimalSteps: 1. Create knyt_personas + qripto_personas tables with the DDL above; enable RLS with auth.uid() = user_id policies on all CRUD ops.
2. Create user_connections table for OAuth + wallet bindings (service-keyed JSONB).

3. Configure LinkedIn OAuth app — register https://<your-supabase>.supabase.co/functions/v1/oauth-callback-linkedin as the redirect URI; store LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID + LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET as edge function secrets.
4. Deploy the two edge functions: connect-linkedin (initiator) and oauth-callback-linkedin (token exchange + profile fetch + redirect).
5. Implement client-side OAuthCallback page that parses connection_data from URL and upserts into user_connections.

6. Implement walletConnectionService + knytTokenService — call eth_requestAccounts, personal_sign, then eth_call balanceOf for the KNYT ERC-20 (0xe53dad36cd0A8EdC656448CE7912bba72beBECb4) on chainId 0x1.
7. Implement processLinkedInConnection + processWalletConnection mappers (non-destructive writes; KNYT-COYN gated by persona detection heuristic).
8. Wire blakQubeService.updatePersonaFromConnections(personaType) to fan out connection_data → persona row updates after every OAuth/wallet event.
9. Add the 30s in-memory balance cache and pendingFetches dedup map to avoid RPC spam.
10. Dispatch CustomEvents (privateDataUpdated, personaDataUpdated, balanceUpdated, walletDataRefreshed) so UI re-renders without polling.
criticalGotchas: Persona-type detection MUST happen before writing KNYT-COYN-Owned — otherwise Qripto personas will get KNYT fields they don't have columns for.
Balance cache key MUST be lowercased wallet address — checksummed addresses cause cache misses.
LinkedIn redirect_uri at token-exchange time MUST byte-match the redirect_uri registered in LinkedIn's app config — any trailing slash or protocol mismatch fails silently with a 400.
Use a 15s AbortController on the LinkedIn token exchange — LinkedIn's OAuth endpoint occasionally hangs.
Always validate chainId === 0x1 BEFORE calling balanceOf — calling on the wrong network returns 0 silently.
Smart-detect the client origin in the OAuth callback (referer → origin → fallback) so Lovable preview URLs and production domains both work.
